title: Shaver motor speed control
abstract: The invention is related to a drive control for driving a dc motor of an electrical household appliance, in particular a hair cutting device such as an electric razor, shaver or epilator, at constant rotational speed. The drive control comprises a detection means for determining a parameter indicative of the actual rotational speed of the dc motor and a closed loop control for adjusting a supply voltage to the dc motor based on a control deviation between a target value and the actual value of the parameter indicative of the rotational speed of the dc motor. In order to provide constant motor speed in cases with varying load and with constant (not varying and in particular no) load, the loop control has a fast control and a slow control for adjusting the supply voltage with the fast control providing a faster correction of the control deviation than the slow control. The loop control further comprises a processor means for determining a switching criterion for switching between the fast control and the slow control, wherein the close loop control is adapted for switching between the fast control and the slow control based on the switching criterion. The present invention further relates to an electric hair and/or skin treating device.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. Drive control for driving a dc motor (M) of an electrical household appliance, in particular a hair cutting device (300) such as an electric razor, shaver or epilator, at constant rotational speed, the drive control comprising - a detection means (31) for determining a parameter indicative of the actual rotational speed of the dc motor (M) and - a closed loop control (100) for adjusting a supply voltage (U m ) to the dc motor based (M) on a control deviation between a target value and the actual value of the parameter indicative of the rotational speed of the dc motor; characterized in that the closed loop control (100) has - a fast control and a slow control for adjusting the supply voltage with the fast control providing a faster correction of the control deviation than the slow control, - a processor means (30) for determining a switching criterion for switching between the fast control and the slow control, wherein at least one switching criterion from the slow control to the fast control and at least one switching criterion from the fast control to the slow control are provided, and wherein the close loop control (100) is adapted for switching between the fast control and the slow control based on the switching criteria. 2. Drive control according to claim 1 , characterized in that the switching criterion is derived from the parameter indicative of the actual rotational speed. 3. Drive control according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that the switching criterion comprises a threshold value for the control deviation (D). 4. Drive control according to any of the preceding claims, characterized in that the fast control is able to deliver a larger impact on the rotational speed of the dc motor (M) per time unit than the slow control. 5. Drive control according to any of the preceding claims, characterized in that a control output of the fast control has a term proportional, integrative and/or derivative to the control deviation (D). 6. Drive control according to any of the preceding claims, characterized in that the change rate of the rotational speed per time unit that can be caused by the slow control is limited by a predetermined maximum change rate. 7. Drive control according to claim 6, characterized in that the control output of the slow control provides discrete limited steps. 8. Drive control according to any of the preceding claims, characterized in that a slow loop frequency of the slow control is less than a fast loop frequency of the fast control. 9. Drive control according to any of the preceding claims, characterized in that the closed loop control comprises a pulse width modulation (PWM) module for adjusting the supply voltage (Um) to the dc motor (M). 10. Drive control according to any of the preceding claims, characterized in that the parameter indicative of the actual rotational speed of the dc motor (M) is the induced motor voltage (Uemf) and/or the rotational speed defined as rotations per time. 11. Drive control according to claim 10, characterized in that the detection means for determining a parameter indicative of the actual rotational speed of the dc motor comprises means (31) for determining the dc voltage of the power source of the electrical appliance and/or means (30) for determining the motor current. 12. Electric hair and/or skin treating device having a main body (301) with a dc-motor (M) for driving a treating tool (303), a power source (2) and a drive control (1) according to any one of the claims 1 to 11. 13. Electric hair and/or skin treating device according to claim 12, characterized in that the treating tool (303) is a modular element detachable and re-attachable to the electric hair and/or skin treating device (300) by coupling the treating tool (303) to a drive mechanism (302) of the electrical hair and/or skin treating device (300) driven by the dc-motor (M). 14. Electric hair and/or skin treating device according to claim 13, characterized in that the modular element (303) comprises a brush, beard trimmer, a body groomer and/or a shaver. 15. Electric hair and/or skin treating device according to any one of the claims 12 to 14, characterized in that the drive control (1) is adapted to control the rotational speed irrespective of the load to an adjustable target rotational speed.
